title: Google implements video selfie verification to recover accounts
summary: The company allows users to use a video selfie as an alternative method to log in or recover blocked accounts.

title: AMD introduces Instinct MI455X accelerator and Helios system to compete in AI
summary: AMD launches its new AI accelerator Instinct MI455X with HBM4 memory and the Helios rack, along with the CDNA 5 architecture, to take on Nvidia.

title: Microsoft launches free Xbox Cloud Gaming with ads and one-hour sessions
summary: Users can play in the cloud without a subscription in exchange for watching ads, with sessions limited to one hour.

title: Black Forest Labs presents Flux 3, its multimodal video and audio generation model
summary: Flux 3 generates clips of up to 20 seconds with native audio, being the first model from Black Forest Labs to offer this capability natively.

title: AMD introduces X100 chips with Strix Halo architecture for robots and physical AI systems
summary: AMD X100 processors integrate Zen 5 cores and RDNA 3.5 for AI applications in robots, competing with Intel Panther Lake.

title: AMD announces EPYC Venice processors with up to 256 cores and 1,152 MB of 3D V-Cache
summary: The EPYC 9006 and EPYC 9996 Venice series, based on Zen 6, offers up to 256 cores, 1,152 MB of L3 cache, and frequencies above 5 GHz.

title: NVIDIA increases price of GDDR7 and GDDR6 memory kits, raising GPU costs
summary: Graphics card manufacturers expect price increases after NVIDIA raises prices on memory modules for its GeForce RTX GPUs.

title: European Union fines Google €890 million for two DMA violations
summary: The European Commission sanctions Google for violating the Digital Markets Act, with a fine totaling €890 million.

title: Geekbench 7 introduces the biggest update with real CPU tests and AI benchmarks
summary: The new version includes CPU tests based on real applications, new multimedia workloads, artificial intelligence benchmarks, and CUDA support.

title: Samsung presents Galaxy Z Fold8, Watch8, and Watch Ultra in its new generation
summary: Samsung launches its new foldable devices and smartwatches, including the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra and the Galaxy Watch8 and Watch Ultra.

title: Sony confirms it will eliminate physical discs in 2028 and censors employees who criticize the decision
summary: Former God of War developer states Sony will not change its mind about eliminating physical discs in 2028, while the company imposes rules on its employees on social media.

title: PlayStation 6 controller will use TMR magnetic sticks to eliminate drifting
summary: Sony will adopt TMR sensors in the PS6 joysticks to end the drift problem of current controllers.

title: Augmental MouthPad allows controlling devices with the tongue via a Bluetooth oral trackpad
summary: The device sits on the palate and works as a trackpad to control smartphones, tablets, and PCs with the tongue.

title: Meta launches optimistic AI ad with a song about the end of the world
summary: Meta's advertising campaign shows an optimistic tone about AI but uses a song that talks about human extinction, generating controversy.

title: OpenAI deploys ChatGPT Health in the US while free medical advice worsens
summary: The ChatGPT Health platform launches for all users in the US, while the free version reduces the quality of health advice to incentivize subscription.

title: Microsoft forces LG to remove McAfee pop-ups in its monitor app
summary: Microsoft acts against bloatware in Windows 11 by forcing LG to remove McAfee pop-ups in its monitor application.

title: RTX 3090 and RTX 3050 combined with Lossless Scaling to achieve 144 FPS at 4K in games
summary: Using the Lossless Scaling technique, two Ampere GPUs manage to double performance in games at 4K, reaching 144 FPS.

title: Microsoft introduces Xbox backward compatibility on PC with four classic games
summary: The first original Xbox games are now available on PC via emulation, using the XeFu Xbox 360 emulator.

title: Intel and AMD sign CPU deals with Chinese customers as prices rise more than 40%
summary: To cope with rising prices, Chinese customers sign long-term agreements with Intel and AMD that guarantee purchase volumes without setting prices.

title: Framework doubles LPCAMM2 memory price and cuts capacity in Laptop 13 Pro
summary: The increased cost of LPCAMM2 modules forces Framework to double prices and reduce RAM in some pre-orders.

title: Steam allows guests to buy games as gifts without needing an account
summary: Valve changes the gift policy on Steam so that anyone can purchase a game as a gift even if they don't have a Steam account.
